what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record that registers an image asset (image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-29) under the design-source channel with an attachment type of images and an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-29
No body content after the frontmatter
conventions: Follows a YAML frontmatter metadata record format
Uses a custom image:// URI scheme to address the attached asset
Includes an ISO 8601 timestamp for when the attachment was added
concerns: The image:// target cannot be verified from this file alone
No caption, alt text, or description of the image is provided
attachment_type is plural ('images') while only one target is listed
notes: A timestamped metadata stub that links the design-source channel to a single image asset under recent-pitches/p-29.
questions: What visual content is in image p-29?
Who added this attachment and for which pitch or decision?
Is there accompanying caption or annotation metadata elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — no explicit stated purpose beyond the attachment metadata, which is consistent with recording an image reference.
intention_signals: It appears intended to attach a specific pitch-related image to the design-source channel so it can be referenced or retrieved as part of channel memory.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme and a dedicated channel attachment id for an image reference.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped mapping between attachment id att-0043034a, the design-source channel, and the image target p-29, preserving the existence and addition time of an image asset in channel memory.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML attachment record linking the design-source channel to image asset p-29 in recent-pitches with an images type and a 2026-06-22 timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register a pitch-related image attachment in the design-source channel for later reference.
